paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces efficient foundation language models trained on trillions of tokens using publicly available datasets, leveraging Chinchilla scaling laws and optimized architectures like pre-normalization, SwiGLU activation, and rotary positional embeddings. Demonstrates competitive performance with larger models despite being significantly smaller
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Common Crawl (67%), C4 (15%), Wikipedia (4.5%), Books (4.5%), ArXiv (2.5%), Stack Exchange (2%), Github (4.5%)
raw_extraction.application: General-purpose language tasks including reasoning, question answering, code generation, and multitask learning
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scalable, efficient, and open-source large language models
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Discriminative fine-tuning for specific tasks (e.g., instruction tuning)
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces efficient foundation language models trained on trillions of tokens using publicly available datasets, leveraging Chinchilla scaling laws and optimized architectures like pre-normalization, SwiGLU activation, and rotary positional embeddings. Demonstrates superior performance relative to GPT-3 despite being 10× smaller
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Common Crawl (67%), C4 (15%), Wikipedia (4.5%), Books (4.5%), ArXiv (2.5%), Stack Exchange (2%), Github (4.5%)
raw_extraction.application: General-purpose language tasks including reasoning, question answering, code generation, and multitask learning
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scalable, efficient, and open-source large language models
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Discriminative fine-tuning for specific tasks (e.g., instruction tuning)
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Optimized transformer architecture with pre-normalization, SwiGLU activation, and rotary positional embeddings enables efficient training and inference across varying scales. Achieves strong performance comparable to much larger models
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Common Crawl (67%), C4 (15%), Wikipedia (4.5%), Books (4.5%), ArXiv (2.5%), Stack Exchange (2%), Github (4.5%)
raw_extraction.application: Advanced reasoning, complex question answering, and multilingual capabilities
raw_extraction.research_problem: Efficient scaling of large language models with minimal compute
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Combines Chinchilla scaling principles with optimized transformer architecture (pre-normalization, SwiGLU, RoPE) to achieve competitive performance against proprietary models like PaLM-540B. Demonstrates that smaller models trained longer can outperform larger ones with equivalent compute budgets
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Common Crawl (67%), C4 (15%), Wikipedia (4.5%), Books (4.5%), ArXiv (2.5%), Stack Exchange (2%), Github (4.5%)
raw_extraction.application: High-capacity reasoning, advanced question answering, and multilingual applications
raw_extraction.research_problem: Balancing model size and performance with open-source constraints
raw_extraction.hardware_used: Single NVIDIA A100 GPU (80GB RAM) for training; optimized inference for distributed systems
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduced an open, efficient foundation language model leveraging Transformer decoder architecture with attention optimization techniques (e.g., rotary positional embeddings, mixed precision training). This addresses the challenge of balancing model scale and computational efficiency while maintaining strong performance
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Various large-scale text corpora (details inferred from context, including references to datasets like Common Crawl, Pile, etc.)
raw_extraction.application: General language understanding, text generation, instruction following, and multi-task learning
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scalable, efficient foundation language models for general-purpose language understanding and generation
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduced larger model sizes while maintaining efficiency through architectural optimizations, demonstrating improved capabilities across various tasks (shown in MMLU evaluations)
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (likely similar to base LLaMA)
raw_extraction.application: Advanced natural language understanding and generation, research applications
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scalability and performance improvements in large language models
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces Chinchilla sampling technique which optimizes pretraining efficiency by balancing sample diversity and computational cost. Demonstrates superior performance-per-dollar compared to standard pretraining methods while maintaining open-source accessibility
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Optimized Chinchilla sampling strategy applied to custom filtered web text corpus
raw_extraction.application: Advanced language understanding, specialized domain adaptation, high-capacity reasoning
raw_extraction.research_problem: High-performance, cost-efficient large language model pretraining
raw_extraction.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
